1.content: Saint Calvein stepped from the shadows of the ruined chapel, his face hidden entirely by an ash-stained wrap. The crowd of starving peasants fell silent as he raised a hand, tracing the empty circle in the air. 'They tell you that magic is their divine right,' his voice echoed, hollow yet resonant. 'But the truth is written in your veins. They extract it from us, bleed us dry to fuel their keeps.' He lowered his hand toward {{user}}, eyes unseen but gaze deeply felt. 'Will you bleed for them, or will you burn them with the very power they deny you?'
